title: Ethereum Working Group Launches Clear Signing to Prevent Blind Signing Risks
summary: A new Ethereum standard called Clear Signing aims to prevent blind signing, a flaw that has led to billions in losses. The initiative involves major wallet developers, security firms, and the Ethereum Foundation.
body:
A coalition of Ethereum wallet developers, security firms, and the Ethereum Foundation’s Trillion Dollar Security Initiative has launched Clear Signing, an open standard designed to prevent blind signing. Blind signing occurs when users approve transactions without fully understanding what they are signing, leading to significant financial losses, including the recent Bybit hack.

Clear Signing provides users with clear, readable information about the transactions they are approving. This includes details about the actions the transaction will perform, the assets involved, and any potential risks. The standard is being adopted by major Ethereum wallets and is expected to significantly reduce the risk of blind signing-related losses.

This initiative is crucial for everyday Ethereum users, as it makes transaction approvals safer and more transparent. By providing clear information, users can make more informed decisions, reducing the likelihood of falling victim to scams or unintended transactions. The standard is part of a broader effort to enhance security across the Ethereum ecosystem.

The Ethereum Foundation and participating wallet developers are encouraging all wallet providers to adopt Clear Signing. Users should look for wallets that support this standard and update their software to benefit from the enhanced security features. The adoption of Clear Signing is a significant step forward in making Ethereum transactions safer for everyone.

Users should watch for updates from their wallet providers about Clear Signing support and ensure they are using the latest versions of their wallets. This will help them take advantage of the new security features and reduce the risk of blind signing-related losses. Read more → https://blog.ethereum.org/en/2026/05/12/clear-signing-announcement

Update your wallet to support Clear Signing and stay safe. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-05-13-ethereum-working-group-launches-clear-signing-to-prevent-blind-signing-risks
